HAMLET, Prince of Denmark

The GHOST of the late king, Hamlet’s father
GERTRUDE, the Queen, Hamlet’s mother, now wife of Claudius
POLONIUS, Lord Chamberlain
LAERTES, Son to Polonius
OPHELIA, Daughter to Polonius
HORATIO, Friend to Hamlet
FORTINBRAS, Prince of Norway
VOLTEMAND, Courtier
CORNELIUS, Courtier
ROSENCRANTZ, Courtier
GUILDENSTERN, Courtier
MARCELLUS, Officer
BARNARDO, Officer
FRANCISCO, a Soldier
OSRIC, Courtier
REYNALDO, Servant to Polonius
Players
A Gentleman, Courtier

English Ambassadors.
Lords, Ladies, Officers, Soldiers, Sailors, Messengers, and Attendants
SCENE. Elsinore.

entities.title: SCENE I. Elsinore. A platform before the Castle.
entities.content: Elsinore. A platform before the Castle.
Enter Francisco and Barnardo, two sentinels.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Who’s there?

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
Nay, answer me. Stand and unfold yourself.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Long live the King!

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
Barnardo?

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
He.

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
You come most carefully upon your hour.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
’Tis now struck twelve. Get thee to bed, Francisco.

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
For this relief much thanks. ’Tis bitter cold,
And I am sick at heart.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Have you had quiet guard?

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
Not a mouse stirring.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Well, good night.
If you do meet Horatio and Marcellus,
The rivals of my watch, bid them make haste.
Enter Horatio and Marcellus.

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
I think I hear them. Stand, ho! Who is there?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Friends to this ground.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
And liegemen to the Dane.

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
Give you good night.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
O, farewell, honest soldier, who hath reliev’d you?

entities.title: FRANCISCO
entities.content:
FRANCISCO.
Barnardo has my place. Give you good-night.
[Exit.]

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Holla, Barnardo!

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Say, what, is Horatio there?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
A piece of him.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Welcome, Horatio. Welcome, good Marcellus.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
What, has this thing appear’d again tonight?

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
I have seen nothing.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Horatio says ’tis but our fantasy,
And will not let belief take hold of him
Touching this dreaded sight, twice seen of us.
Therefore I have entreated him along
With us to watch the minutes of this night,
That if again this apparition come
He may approve our eyes and speak to it.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Tush, tush, ’twill not appear.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Sit down awhile,
And let us once again assail your ears,
That are so fortified against our story,
What we two nights have seen.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Well, sit we down,
And let us hear Barnardo speak of this.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Last night of all,
When yond same star that’s westward from the pole,
Had made his course t’illume that part of heaven
Where now it burns, Marcellus and myself,
The bell then beating one—

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Peace, break thee off. Look where it comes again.
Enter Ghost.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
In the same figure, like the King that’s dead.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Thou art a scholar; speak to it, Horatio.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
Looks it not like the King? Mark it, Horatio.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Most like. It harrows me with fear and wonder.

BARNARDO
It would be spoke to.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Question it, Horatio.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What art thou that usurp’st this time of night,
Together with that fair and warlike form
In which the majesty of buried Denmark
Did sometimes march? By heaven I charge thee speak.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
It is offended.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
See, it stalks away.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Stay! speak, speak! I charge thee speak!
[Exit Ghost.]

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
’Tis gone, and will not answer.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
How now, Horatio! You tremble and look pale.
Is not this something more than fantasy?
What think you on’t?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Before my God, I might not this believe
Without the sensible and true avouch
Of mine own eyes.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Is it not like the King?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
As thou art to thyself:
Such was the very armour he had on
When he th’ambitious Norway combated;
So frown’d he once, when in an angry parle
He smote the sledded Polacks on the ice.
’Tis strange.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Thus twice before, and jump at this dead hour,
With martial stalk hath he gone by our watch.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
In what particular thought to work I know not;
But in the gross and scope of my opinion,
This bodes some strange eruption to our state.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Good now, sit down, and tell me, he that knows,
Why this same strict and most observant watch
So nightly toils the subject of the land,
And why such daily cast of brazen cannon
And foreign mart for implements of war;
Why such impress of shipwrights, whose sore task
Does not divide the Sunday from the week.
What might be toward, that this sweaty haste
Doth make the night joint-labourer with the day:
Who is’t that can inform me?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
That can I;
At least, the whisper goes so. Our last King,
Whose image even but now appear’d to us,
Was, as you know, by Fortinbras of Norway,
Thereto prick’d on by a most emulate pride,
Dar’d to the combat; in which our valiant Hamlet,
For so this side of our known world esteem’d him,
Did slay this Fortinbras; who by a seal’d compact,
Well ratified by law and heraldry,
Did forfeit, with his life, all those his lands
Which he stood seiz’d of, to the conqueror;
Against the which, a moiety competent
Was gaged by our King; which had return’d
To the inheritance of Fortinbras,
Had he been vanquisher; as by the same cov’nant
And carriage of the article design’d,
His fell to Hamlet. Now, sir, young Fortinbras,
Of unimproved mettle, hot and full,
Hath in the skirts of Norway, here and there,
Shark’d up a list of lawless resolutes,
For food and diet, to some enterprise
That hath a stomach in’t; which is no other,
As it doth well appear unto our state,
But to recover of us by strong hand
And terms compulsatory, those foresaid lands
So by his father lost. And this, I take it,
Is the main motive of our preparations,
The source of this our watch, and the chief head
Of this post-haste and rummage in the land.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
I think it be no other but e’en so:
Well may it sort that this portentous figure
Comes armed through our watch so like the King
That was and is the question of these wars.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
A mote it is to trouble the mind’s eye.
In the most high and palmy state of Rome,
A little ere the mightiest Julius fell,
The graves stood tenantless and the sheeted dead
Did squeak and gibber in the Roman streets;
As stars with trains of fire and dews of blood,
Disasters in the sun; and the moist star,
Upon whose influence Neptune’s empire stands,
Was sick almost to doomsday with eclipse.
And even the like precurse of fierce events,
As harbingers preceding still the fates
And prologue to the omen coming on,
Have heaven and earth together demonstrated
Unto our climatures and countrymen.

Re-enter Ghost.

But, soft, behold! Lo, where it comes again!
I’ll cross it, though it blast me. Stay, illusion!
If thou hast any sound, or use of voice,
Speak to me.
If there be any good thing to be done,
That may to thee do ease, and grace to me,
Speak to me.
If thou art privy to thy country’s fate,
Which, happily, foreknowing may avoid,
O speak!
Or if thou hast uphoarded in thy life
Extorted treasure in the womb of earth,
For which, they say, you spirits oft walk in death,
Speak of it. Stay, and speak!

[The cock crows.]

Stop it, Marcellus!

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Shall I strike at it with my partisan?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Do, if it will not stand.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
’Tis here!

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
’Tis here!
[Exit Ghost.]

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
’Tis gone!
We do it wrong, being so majestical,
To offer it the show of violence,
For it is as the air, invulnerable,
And our vain blows malicious mockery.

entities.title: BARNARDO
entities.content:
BARNARDO.
It was about to speak, when the cock crew.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
And then it started, like a guilty thing
Upon a fearful summons. I have heard
The cock, that is the trumpet to the morn,
Doth with his lofty and shrill-sounding throat
Awake the god of day; and at his warning,
Whether in sea or fire, in earth or air,
Th’extravagant and erring spirit hies
To his confine. And of the truth herein
This present object made probation.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
It faded on the crowing of the cock.
Some say that ever ’gainst that season comes
Wherein our Saviour’s birth is celebrated,
The bird of dawning singeth all night long;
And then, they say, no spirit dare stir abroad,
The nights are wholesome, then no planets strike,
No fairy takes, nor witch hath power to charm;
So hallow’d and so gracious is the time.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
So have I heard, and do in part believe it.
But look, the morn in russet mantle clad,
Walks o’er the dew of yon high eastward hill.
Break we our watch up, and by my advice,
Let us impart what we have seen tonight
Unto young Hamlet; for upon my life,
This spirit, dumb to us, will speak to him.
Do you consent we shall acquaint him with it,
As needful in our loves, fitting our duty?

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Let’s do’t, I pray, and I this morning know
Where we shall find him most conveniently.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE II. Elsinore. A room of state in the Castle.
entities.content: Elsinore. A room of state in the Castle.
Enter Claudius King of Denmark, Gertrude the Queen, Hamlet, Polonius, Laertes, Voltemand, Cornelius, Lords and Attendant.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Though yet of Hamlet our dear brother’s death
The memory be green, and that it us befitted
To bear our hearts in grief, and our whole kingdom
To be contracted in one brow of woe;
Yet so far hath discretion fought with nature
That we with wisest sorrow think on him,
Together with remembrance of ourselves.
Therefore our sometime sister, now our queen,
Th’imperial jointress to this warlike state,
Have we, as ’twere with a defeated joy,
With one auspicious and one dropping eye,
With mirth in funeral, and with dirge in marriage,
In equal scale weighing delight and dole,
Taken to wife; nor have we herein barr’d
Your better wisdoms, which have freely gone
With this affair along. For all, our thanks.
Now follows, that you know young Fortinbras,
Holding a weak supposal of our worth,
Or thinking by our late dear brother’s death
Our state to be disjoint and out of frame,
Colleagued with this dream of his advantage,
He hath not fail’d to pester us with message,
Importing the surrender of those lands
Lost by his father, with all bonds of law,
To our most valiant brother. So much for him.
Now for ourself and for this time of meeting:
Thus much the business is: we have here writ
To Norway, uncle of young Fortinbras,
Who, impotent and bed-rid, scarcely hears
Of this his nephew’s purpose, to suppress
His further gait herein; in that the levies,
The lists, and full proportions are all made
Out of his subject: and we here dispatch
You, good Cornelius, and you, Voltemand,
For bearers of this greeting to old Norway,
Giving to you no further personal power
To business with the King, more than the scope
Of these dilated articles allow.
Farewell; and let your haste commend your duty.

entities.title: CORNELIUS AND VOLTEMAND
entities.content:
CORNELIUS and VOLTEMAND.
In that, and all things, will we show our duty.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
We doubt it nothing: heartily farewell.

[Exeunt Voltemand and Cornelius.]

And now, Laertes, what’s the news with you?
You told us of some suit. What is’t, Laertes?
You cannot speak of reason to the Dane,
And lose your voice. What wouldst thou beg, Laertes,
That shall not be my offer, not thy asking?
The head is not more native to the heart,
The hand more instrumental to the mouth,
Than is the throne of Denmark to thy father.
What wouldst thou have, Laertes?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Dread my lord,
Your leave and favour to return to France,
From whence though willingly I came to Denmark
To show my duty in your coronation;
Yet now I must confess, that duty done,
My thoughts and wishes bend again toward France,
And bow them to your gracious leave and pardon.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Have you your father’s leave? What says Polonius?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
He hath, my lord, wrung from me my slow leave
By laboursome petition; and at last
Upon his will I seal’d my hard consent.
I do beseech you give him leave to go.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Take thy fair hour, Laertes; time be thine,
And thy best graces spend it at thy will!
But now, my cousin Hamlet, and my son—

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
[Aside.] A little more than kin, and less than kind.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
How is it that the clouds still hang on you?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Not so, my lord, I am too much i’ the sun.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Good Hamlet, cast thy nighted colour off,
And let thine eye look like a friend on Denmark.
Do not for ever with thy vailed lids
Seek for thy noble father in the dust.
Thou know’st ’tis common, all that lives must die,
Passing through nature to eternity.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Ay, madam, it is common.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
If it be,
Why seems it so particular with thee?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Seems, madam! Nay, it is; I know not seems.
’Tis not alone my inky cloak, good mother,
Nor customary suits of solemn black,
Nor windy suspiration of forc’d breath,
No, nor the fruitful river in the eye,
Nor the dejected haviour of the visage,
Together with all forms, moods, shows of grief,
That can denote me truly. These indeed seem,
For they are actions that a man might play;
But I have that within which passeth show;
These but the trappings and the suits of woe.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
’Tis sweet and commendable in your nature, Hamlet,
To give these mourning duties to your father;
But you must know, your father lost a father,
That father lost, lost his, and the survivor bound
In filial obligation, for some term
To do obsequious sorrow. But to persevere
In obstinate condolement is a course
Of impious stubbornness. ’Tis unmanly grief,
It shows a will most incorrect to heaven,
A heart unfortified, a mind impatient,
An understanding simple and unschool’d;
For what we know must be, and is as common
As any the most vulgar thing to sense,
Why should we in our peevish opposition
Take it to heart? Fie, ’tis a fault to heaven,
A fault against the dead, a fault to nature,
To reason most absurd, whose common theme
Is death of fathers, and who still hath cried,
From the first corse till he that died today,
‘This must be so.’ We pray you throw to earth
This unprevailing woe, and think of us
As of a father; for let the world take note
You are the most immediate to our throne,
And with no less nobility of love
Than that which dearest father bears his son
Do I impart toward you. For your intent
In going back to school in Wittenberg,
It is most retrograde to our desire:
And we beseech you bend you to remain
Here in the cheer and comfort of our eye,
Our chiefest courtier, cousin, and our son.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Let not thy mother lose her prayers, Hamlet.
I pray thee stay with us; go not to Wittenberg.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I shall in all my best obey you, madam.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Why, ’tis a loving and a fair reply.
Be as ourself in Denmark. Madam, come;
This gentle and unforc’d accord of Hamlet
Sits smiling to my heart; in grace whereof,
No jocund health that Denmark drinks today
But the great cannon to the clouds shall tell,
And the King’s rouse the heaven shall bruit again,
Re-speaking earthly thunder. Come away.
[Exeunt all but Hamlet.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O that this too too solid flesh would melt,
Thaw, and resolve itself into a dew!
Or that the Everlasting had not fix’d
His canon ’gainst self-slaughter. O God! O God!
How weary, stale, flat, and unprofitable
Seem to me all the uses of this world!
Fie on’t! Oh fie! ’tis an unweeded garden
That grows to seed; things rank and gross in nature
Possess it merely. That it should come to this!
But two months dead—nay, not so much, not two:
So excellent a king; that was to this
Hyperion to a satyr; so loving to my mother,
That he might not beteem the winds of heaven
Visit her face too roughly. Heaven and earth!
Must I remember? Why, she would hang on him
As if increase of appetite had grown
By what it fed on; and yet, within a month—
Let me not think on’t—Frailty, thy name is woman!
A little month, or ere those shoes were old
With which she followed my poor father’s body
Like Niobe, all tears.—Why she, even she—
O God! A beast that wants discourse of reason
Would have mourn’d longer,—married with mine uncle,
My father’s brother; but no more like my father
Than I to Hercules. Within a month,
Ere yet the salt of most unrighteous tears
Had left the flushing in her galled eyes,
She married. O most wicked speed, to post
With such dexterity to incestuous sheets!
It is not, nor it cannot come to good.
But break, my heart, for I must hold my tongue.
Enter Horatio, Marcellus and Barnardo.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Hail to your lordship!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I am glad to see you well:
Horatio, or I do forget myself.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
The same, my lord,
And your poor servant ever.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Sir, my good friend;
I’ll change that name with you:
And what make you from Wittenberg, Horatio?—
Marcellus?

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
My good lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I am very glad to see you.—Good even, sir.—
But what, in faith, make you from Wittenberg?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
A truant disposition, good my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I would not hear your enemy say so;
Nor shall you do my ear that violence,
To make it truster of your own report
Against yourself. I know you are no truant.
But what is your affair in Elsinore?
We’ll teach you to drink deep ere you depart.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
My lord, I came to see your father’s funeral.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I prithee do not mock me, fellow-student.
I think it was to see my mother’s wedding.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Indeed, my lord, it follow’d hard upon.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Thrift, thrift, Horatio! The funeral bak’d meats
Did coldly furnish forth the marriage tables.
Would I had met my dearest foe in heaven
Or ever I had seen that day, Horatio.
My father,—methinks I see my father.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Where, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
In my mind’s eye, Horatio.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
I saw him once; he was a goodly king.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
He was a man, take him for all in all,
I shall not look upon his like again.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
My lord, I think I saw him yesternight.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Saw? Who?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
My lord, the King your father.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The King my father!

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Season your admiration for a while
With an attent ear, till I may deliver
Upon the witness of these gentlemen
This marvel to you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
For God’s love let me hear.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Two nights together had these gentlemen,
Marcellus and Barnardo, on their watch
In the dead waste and middle of the night,
Been thus encounter’d. A figure like your father,
Armed at point exactly, cap-à-pie,
Appears before them, and with solemn march
Goes slow and stately by them: thrice he walk’d
By their oppress’d and fear-surprised eyes,
Within his truncheon’s length; whilst they, distill’d
Almost to jelly with the act of fear,
Stand dumb, and speak not to him. This to me
In dreadful secrecy impart they did,
And I with them the third night kept the watch,
Where, as they had deliver’d, both in time,
Form of the thing, each word made true and good,
The apparition comes. I knew your father;
These hands are not more like.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
But where was this?

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
My lord, upon the platform where we watch.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Did you not speak to it?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
My lord, I did;
But answer made it none: yet once methought
It lifted up it head, and did address
Itself to motion, like as it would speak.
But even then the morning cock crew loud,
And at the sound it shrunk in haste away,
And vanish’d from our sight.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
’Tis very strange.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
As I do live, my honour’d lord, ’tis true;
And we did think it writ down in our duty
To let you know of it.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Indeed, indeed, sirs, but this troubles me.
Hold you the watch tonight?

entities.title: MARCELLUS AND BARNARDO
entities.content:
MARCELLUS and BARNARDO.
We do, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Arm’d, say you?

Both.
Arm’d, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
From top to toe?

entities.title: BOTH
entities.content:
BOTH.
My lord, from head to foot.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then saw you not his face?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
O yes, my lord, he wore his beaver up.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What, look’d he frowningly?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
A countenance more in sorrow than in anger.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Pale, or red?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Nay, very pale.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
And fix’d his eyes upon you?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Most constantly.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I would I had been there.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It would have much amaz’d you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Very like, very like. Stay’d it long?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
While one with moderate haste might tell a hundred.

entities.title: MARCELLUS AND BARNARDO
entities.content:
MARCELLUS and BARNARDO.
Longer, longer.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Not when I saw’t.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
His beard was grizzled, no?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It was, as I have seen it in his life,
A sable silver’d.

entities.title: HAMLET
HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
I warrant you it will.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
If it assume my noble father’s person,
I’ll speak to it, though hell itself should gape
And bid me hold my peace. I pray you all,
If you have hitherto conceal’d this sight,
Let it be tenable in your silence still;
And whatsoever else shall hap tonight,
Give it an understanding, but no tongue.
I will requite your loves. So, fare ye well.
Upon the platform ’twixt eleven and twelve,
I’ll visit you.

entities.title: ALL
entities.content:
ALL.
Our duty to your honour.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Your loves, as mine to you: farewell.

[Exeunt Horatio, Marcellus and Barnardo.]

My father’s spirit in arms! All is not well;
I doubt some foul play: would the night were come!
Till then sit still, my soul: foul deeds will rise,
Though all the earth o’erwhelm them, to men’s eyes.
[Exit.]

entities.title: SCENE III. A room in Polonius’s house.
entities.content: A room in Polonius’s house.
Enter Laertes and Ophelia.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
My necessaries are embark’d. Farewell.
And, sister, as the winds give benefit
And convoy is assistant, do not sleep,
But let me hear from you.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
Do you doubt that?

entities.title: LAERTES
OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
No more but so?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Think it no more.
For nature crescent does not grow alone
In thews and bulk; but as this temple waxes,
The inward service of the mind and soul
Grows wide withal. Perhaps he loves you now,
And now no soil nor cautel doth besmirch
The virtue of his will; but you must fear,
His greatness weigh’d, his will is not his own;
For he himself is subject to his birth:
He may not, as unvalu’d persons do,
Carve for himself; for on his choice depends
The sanctity and health of this whole state;
And therefore must his choice be circumscrib’d
Unto the voice and yielding of that body
Whereof he is the head. Then if he says he loves you,
It fits your wisdom so far to believe it
As he in his particular act and place
May give his saying deed; which is no further
Than the main voice of Denmark goes withal.
Then weigh what loss your honour may sustain
If with too credent ear you list his songs,
Or lose your heart, or your chaste treasure open
To his unmaster’d importunity.
Fear it, Ophelia, fear it, my dear sister;
And keep you in the rear of your affection,
Out of the shot and danger of desire.
The chariest maid is prodigal enough
If she unmask her beauty to the moon.
Virtue itself ’scapes not calumnious strokes:
The canker galls the infants of the spring
Too oft before their buttons be disclos’d,
And in the morn and liquid dew of youth
Contagious blastments are most imminent.
Be wary then, best safety lies in fear.
Youth to itself rebels, though none else near.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
I shall th’effect of this good lesson keep
As watchman to my heart. But good my brother,
Do not as some ungracious pastors do,
Show me the steep and thorny way to heaven;
Whilst like a puff’d and reckless libertine
Himself the primrose path of dalliance treads,
And recks not his own rede.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
O, fear me not.
I stay too long. But here my father comes.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Yet here, Laertes? Aboard, aboard, for shame.
The wind sits in the shoulder of your sail,
And you are stay’d for. There, my blessing with you.

[Laying his hand on Laertes’s head.]

And these few precepts in thy memory
Look thou character. Give thy thoughts no tongue,
Nor any unproportion’d thought his act.
Be thou familiar, but by no means vulgar.
Those friends thou hast, and their adoption tried,
Grapple them unto thy soul with hoops of steel;
But do not dull thy palm with entertainment
Of each new-hatch’d, unfledg’d comrade. Beware
Of entrance to a quarrel; but being in,
Bear’t that th’opposed may beware of thee.
Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice:
Take each man’s censure, but reserve thy judgement.
Costly thy habit as thy purse can buy,
But not express’d in fancy; rich, not gaudy:
For the apparel oft proclaims the man;
And they in France of the best rank and station
Are of a most select and generous chief in that.
Neither a borrower nor a lender be:
For loan oft loses both itself and friend;
And borrowing dulls the edge of husbandry.
This above all: to thine own self be true;
And it must follow, as the night the day,
Thou canst not then be false to any man.
Farewell: my blessing season this in thee.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Most humbly do I take my leave, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
The time invites you; go, your servants tend.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Farewell, Ophelia, and remember well
What I have said to you.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
’Tis in my memory lock’d,
And you yourself shall keep the key of it.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Farewell.
[Exit.]

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
What is’t, Ophelia, he hath said to you?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
So please you, something touching the Lord Hamlet.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Marry, well bethought:
’Tis told me he hath very oft of late
Given private time to you; and you yourself
Have of your audience been most free and bounteous.
If it be so,—as so ’tis put on me,
And that in way of caution,—I must tell you
You do not understand yourself so clearly
As it behoves my daughter and your honour.
What is between you? Give me up the truth.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
He hath, my lord, of late made many tenders
Of his affection to me.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Affection! Pooh! You speak like a green girl,
Unsifted in such perilous circumstance.
Do you believe his tenders, as you call them?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
I do not know, my lord, what I should think.

entities.title: POLONIUS
OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
My lord, he hath importun’d me with love
In honourable fashion.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Ay, fashion you may call it; go to, go to.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
And hath given countenance to his speech, my lord,
With almost all the holy vows of heaven.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Ay, springes to catch woodcocks. I do know,
When the blood burns, how prodigal the soul
Lends the tongue vows: these blazes, daughter,
Giving more light than heat, extinct in both,
Even in their promise, as it is a-making,
You must not take for fire. From this time
Be something scanter of your maiden presence;
Set your entreatments at a higher rate
Than a command to parley. For Lord Hamlet,
Believe so much in him that he is young;
And with a larger tether may he walk
Than may be given you. In few, Ophelia,
Do not believe his vows; for they are brokers,
Not of that dye which their investments show,
But mere implorators of unholy suits,
Breathing like sanctified and pious bawds,
The better to beguile. This is for all:
I would not, in plain terms, from this time forth
Have you so slander any moment leisure
As to give words or talk with the Lord Hamlet.
Look to’t, I charge you; come your ways.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
I shall obey, my lord.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE IV. The platform.
entities.content: The platform.
Enter Hamlet, Horatio and Marcellus.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The air bites shrewdly; it is very cold.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It is a nipping and an eager air.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What hour now?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
I think it lacks of twelve.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
No, it is struck.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Indeed? I heard it not. It then draws near the season
Wherein the spirit held his wont to walk.

[A flourish of trumpets, and ordnance shot off within.]

What does this mean, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The King doth wake tonight and takes his rouse,
Keeps wassail, and the swaggering upspring reels;
And as he drains his draughts of Rhenish down,
The kettle-drum and trumpet thus bray out
The triumph of his pledge.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Is it a custom?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Ay marry is’t;
And to my mind, though I am native here,
And to the manner born, it is a custom
More honour’d in the breach than the observance.
This heavy-headed revel east and west
Makes us traduc’d and tax’d of other nations:
They clepe us drunkards, and with swinish phrase
Soil our addition; and indeed it takes
From our achievements, though perform’d at height,
The pith and marrow of our attribute.
So oft it chances in particular men
That for some vicious mole of nature in them,
As in their birth, wherein they are not guilty,
Since nature cannot choose his origin,
By their o’ergrowth of some complexion,
Oft breaking down the pales and forts of reason;
Or by some habit, that too much o’erleavens
The form of plausive manners;—that these men,
Carrying, I say, the stamp of one defect,
Being Nature’s livery or Fortune’s star,—
His virtues else,—be they as pure as grace,
As infinite as man may undergo,
Shall in the general censure take corruption
From that particular fault. The dram of evil
Doth all the noble substance of a doubt
To his own scandal.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Look, my lord, it comes!
Enter Ghost.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Angels and ministers of grace defend us!
Be thou a spirit of health or goblin damn’d,
Bring with thee airs from heaven or blasts from hell,
Be thy intents wicked or charitable,
Thou com’st in such a questionable shape
That I will speak to thee. I’ll call thee Hamlet,
King, father, royal Dane. O, answer me!
Let me not burst in ignorance; but tell
Why thy canoniz’d bones, hearsed in death,
Have burst their cerements; why the sepulchre,
Wherein we saw thee quietly inurn’d,
Hath op’d his ponderous and marble jaws
To cast thee up again! What may this mean,
That thou, dead corse, again in complete steel,
Revisit’st thus the glimpses of the moon,
Making night hideous, and we fools of nature
So horridly to shake our disposition
With thoughts beyond the reaches of our souls?
Say, why is this? Wherefore? What should we do?
[Ghost beckons Hamlet.]

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It beckons you to go away with it,
As if it some impartment did desire
To you alone.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Look with what courteous action
It waves you to a more removed ground.
But do not go with it.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
No, by no means.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
It will not speak; then will I follow it.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Do not, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, what should be the fear?
I do not set my life at a pin’s fee;
And for my soul, what can it do to that,
Being a thing immortal as itself?
It waves me forth again. I’ll follow it.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What if it tempt you toward the flood, my lord,
Or to the dreadful summit of the cliff
That beetles o’er his base into the sea,
And there assume some other horrible form
Which might deprive your sovereignty of reason,
And draw you into madness? Think of it.
The very place puts toys of desperation,
Without more motive, into every brain
That looks so many fathoms to the sea
And hears it roar beneath.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
It waves me still.
Go on, I’ll follow thee.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
You shall not go, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Hold off your hands.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Be rul’d; you shall not go.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
My fate cries out,
And makes each petty artery in this body
As hardy as the Nemean lion’s nerve.

[Ghost beckons.]

Still am I call’d. Unhand me, gentlemen.

[Breaking free from them.]

By heaven, I’ll make a ghost of him that lets me.
I say, away!—Go on, I’ll follow thee.
[Exeunt Ghost and Hamlet.]

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
He waxes desperate with imagination.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Let’s follow; ’tis not fit thus to obey him.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Have after. To what issue will this come?

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Something is rotten in the state of Denmark.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Heaven will direct it.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Nay, let’s follow him.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE V. A more remote part of the Castle.
entities.content: A more remote part of the Castle.
Enter Ghost and Hamlet.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Whither wilt thou lead me? Speak, I’ll go no further.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
Mark me.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I will.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
My hour is almost come,
When I to sulph’rous and tormenting flames
Must render up myself.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Alas, poor ghost!

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
Pity me not, but lend thy serious hearing
To what I shall unfold.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Speak, I am bound to hear.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
So art thou to revenge, when thou shalt hear.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What?

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
I am thy father’s spirit,
Doom’d for a certain term to walk the night,
And for the day confin’d to fast in fires,
Till the foul crimes done in my days of nature
Are burnt and purg’d away. But that I am forbid
To tell the secrets of my prison-house,
I could a tale unfold whose lightest word
Would harrow up thy soul; freeze thy young blood,
Make thy two eyes like stars start from their spheres,
Thy knotted and combined locks to part,
And each particular hair to stand on end
Like quills upon the fretful porpentine.
But this eternal blazon must not be
To ears of flesh and blood. List, list, O, list!
If thou didst ever thy dear father love—

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O God!

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
Revenge his foul and most unnatural murder.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Murder!

entities.title: GHOST
HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Haste me to know’t, that I, with wings as swift
As meditation or the thoughts of love
May sweep to my revenge.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
I find thee apt;
And duller shouldst thou be than the fat weed
That rots itself in ease on Lethe wharf,
Wouldst thou not stir in this. Now, Hamlet, hear.
’Tis given out that, sleeping in my orchard,
A serpent stung me; so the whole ear of Denmark
Is by a forged process of my death
Rankly abus’d; but know, thou noble youth,
The serpent that did sting thy father’s life
Now wears his crown.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O my prophetic soul!
Mine uncle!

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
Ay, that incestuous, that adulterate beast,
With witchcraft of his wit, with traitorous gifts,—
O wicked wit, and gifts, that have the power
So to seduce!—won to his shameful lust
The will of my most seeming-virtuous queen.
O Hamlet, what a falling off was there,
From me, whose love was of that dignity
That it went hand in hand even with the vow
I made to her in marriage; and to decline
Upon a wretch whose natural gifts were poor
To those of mine. But virtue, as it never will be mov’d,
Though lewdness court it in a shape of heaven;
So lust, though to a radiant angel link’d,
Will sate itself in a celestial bed
And prey on garbage.
But soft! methinks I scent the morning air;
Brief let me be. Sleeping within my orchard,
My custom always of the afternoon,
Upon my secure hour thy uncle stole
With juice of cursed hebenon in a vial,
And in the porches of my ears did pour
The leperous distilment, whose effect
Holds such an enmity with blood of man
That swift as quicksilver it courses through
The natural gates and alleys of the body;
And with a sudden vigour it doth posset
And curd, like eager droppings into milk,
The thin and wholesome blood. So did it mine;
And a most instant tetter bark’d about,
Most lazar-like, with vile and loathsome crust
All my smooth body.
Thus was I, sleeping, by a brother’s hand,
Of life, of crown, of queen at once dispatch’d:
Cut off even in the blossoms of my sin,
Unhous’led, disappointed, unanel’d;
No reckoning made, but sent to my account
With all my imperfections on my head.
O horrible! O horrible! most horrible!
If thou hast nature in thee, bear it not;
Let not the royal bed of Denmark be
A couch for luxury and damned incest.
But howsoever thou pursu’st this act,
Taint not thy mind, nor let thy soul contrive
Against thy mother aught; leave her to heaven,
And to those thorns that in her bosom lodge,
To prick and sting her. Fare thee well at once!
The glow-worm shows the matin to be near,
And ’gins to pale his uneffectual fire.
Adieu, adieu, adieu. Remember me.
[Exit.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O all you host of heaven! O earth! What else?
And shall I couple hell? O, fie! Hold, my heart;
And you, my sinews, grow not instant old,
But bear me stiffly up. Remember thee?
Ay, thou poor ghost, while memory holds a seat
In this distracted globe. Remember thee?
Yea, from the table of my memory
I’ll wipe away all trivial fond records,
All saws of books, all forms, all pressures past,
That youth and observation copied there;
And thy commandment all alone shall live
Within the book and volume of my brain,
Unmix’d with baser matter. Yes, by heaven!
O most pernicious woman!
O villain, villain, smiling damned villain!
My tables. Meet it is I set it down,
That one may smile, and smile, and be a villain!
At least I am sure it may be so in Denmark.

[Writing.]

So, uncle, there you are. Now to my word;
It is ‘Adieu, adieu, remember me.’
I have sworn’t.

entities.title: HORATIO AND MARCELLUS
entities.content:
HORATIO and MARCELLUS.
[Within.] My lord, my lord.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
[Within.] Lord Hamlet.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
[Within.] Heaven secure him.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
So be it!

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
[Within.] Illo, ho, ho, my lord!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Hillo, ho, ho, boy! Come, bird, come.
Enter Horatio and Marcellus.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
How is’t, my noble lord?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What news, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O, wonderful!

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Good my lord, tell it.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
No, you’ll reveal it.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Not I, my lord, by heaven.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Nor I, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How say you then, would heart of man once think it?—
But you’ll be secret?

entities.title: HORATIO AND MARCELLUS
entities.content:
HORATIO and MARCELLUS.
Ay, by heaven, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
There’s ne’er a villain dwelling in all Denmark
But he’s an arrant knave.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
There needs no ghost, my lord, come from the grave
To tell us this.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, right; you are i’ the right;
And so, without more circumstance at all,
I hold it fit that we shake hands and part:
You, as your business and desire shall point you,—
For every man hath business and desire,
Such as it is;—and for my own poor part,
Look you, I’ll go pray.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
These are but wild and whirling words, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
There’s no offence, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Yes, by Saint Patrick, but there is, Horatio,
And much offence too. Touching this vision here,
It is an honest ghost, that let me tell you.
For your desire to know what is between us,
O’ermaster’t as you may. And now, good friends,
As you are friends, scholars, and soldiers,
Give me one poor request.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What is’t, my lord? We will.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Never make known what you have seen tonight.

entities.title: HORATIO AND MARCELLUS
entities.content:
HORATIO and MARCELLUS.
My lord, we will not.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Nay, but swear’t.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
In faith, my lord, not I.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
Nor I, my lord, in faith.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Upon my sword.

entities.title: MARCELLUS
entities.content:
MARCELLUS.
We have sworn, my lord, already.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Indeed, upon my sword, indeed.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
[Cries under the stage.] Swear.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Ha, ha boy, sayst thou so? Art thou there, truepenny?
Come on, you hear this fellow in the cellarage.
Consent to swear.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Propose the oath, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Never to speak of this that you have seen.
Swear by my sword.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
[Beneath.] Swear.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Hic et ubique? Then we’ll shift our ground.
Come hither, gentlemen,
And lay your hands again upon my sword.
Never to speak of this that you have heard.
Swear by my sword.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
[Beneath.] Swear.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Well said, old mole! Canst work i’ th’earth so fast?
A worthy pioner! Once more remove, good friends.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
O day and night, but this is wondrous strange.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
And therefore as a stranger give it welcome.
There are more things in heaven and earth, Horatio,
Than are dreamt of in your philosophy. But come,
Here, as before, never, so help you mercy,
How strange or odd soe’er I bear myself,—
As I perchance hereafter shall think meet
To put an antic disposition on—
That you, at such times seeing me, never shall,
With arms encumber’d thus, or this head-shake,
Or by pronouncing of some doubtful phrase,
As ‘Well, we know’, or ‘We could and if we would’,
Or ‘If we list to speak’; or ‘There be and if they might’,
Or such ambiguous giving out, to note
That you know aught of me:—this not to do.
So grace and mercy at your most need help you,
Swear.

entities.title: GHOST
entities.content:
GHOST.
[Beneath.] Swear.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Rest, rest, perturbed spirit. So, gentlemen,
With all my love I do commend me to you;
And what so poor a man as Hamlet is
May do t’express his love and friending to you,
God willing, shall not lack. Let us go in together,
And still your fingers on your lips, I pray.
The time is out of joint. O cursed spite,
That ever I was born to set it right.
Nay, come, let’s go together.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE I. A room in Polonius’s house.
entities.content: A room in Polonius’s house.
Enter Polonius and Reynaldo.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Give him this money and these notes, Reynaldo.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
I will, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
You shall do marvellous wisely, good Reynaldo,
Before you visit him, to make inquiry
Of his behaviour.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
My lord, I did intend it.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Marry, well said; very well said. Look you, sir,
Enquire me first what Danskers are in Paris;
And how, and who, what means, and where they keep,
What company, at what expense; and finding
By this encompassment and drift of question,
That they do know my son, come you more nearer
Than your particular demands will touch it.
Take you as ’twere some distant knowledge of him,
As thus, ‘I know his father and his friends,
And in part him’—do you mark this, Reynaldo?

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
Ay, very well, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
As gaming, my lord?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Ay, or drinking, fencing, swearing,
Quarrelling, drabbing. You may go so far.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
My lord, that would dishonour him.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Faith no, as you may season it in the charge.
You must not put another scandal on him,
That he is open to incontinency;
That’s not my meaning: but breathe his faults so quaintly
That they may seem the taints of liberty;
The flash and outbreak of a fiery mind,
A savageness in unreclaimed blood,
Of general assault.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
But my good lord—

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Wherefore should you do this?

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
Ay, my lord, I would know that.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Marry, sir, here’s my drift,
And I believe it is a fetch of warrant.
You laying these slight sullies on my son,
As ’twere a thing a little soil’d i’ th’ working,
Mark you,
Your party in converse, him you would sound,
Having ever seen in the prenominate crimes
The youth you breathe of guilty, be assur’d
He closes with you in this consequence;
‘Good sir,’ or so; or ‘friend,’ or ‘gentleman’—
According to the phrase or the addition
Of man and country.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
Very good, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
And then, sir, does he this,—
He does—What was I about to say?
By the mass, I was about to say something. Where did I leave?

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
At ‘closes in the consequence.’
At ‘friend or so,’ and ‘gentleman.’

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
At ‘closes in the consequence’ ay, marry!
He closes with you thus: ‘I know the gentleman,
I saw him yesterday, or t’other day,
Or then, or then, with such and such; and, as you say,
There was he gaming, there o’ertook in’s rouse,
There falling out at tennis’: or perchance,
‘I saw him enter such a house of sale’—
Videlicet, a brothel, or so forth. See you now;
Your bait of falsehood takes this carp of truth;
And thus do we of wisdom and of reach,
With windlasses, and with assays of bias,
By indirections find directions out.
So by my former lecture and advice
Shall you my son. You have me, have you not?

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
My lord, I have.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
God b’ wi’ you, fare you well.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
Good my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Observe his inclination in yourself.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
I shall, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
And let him ply his music.

entities.title: REYNALDO
entities.content:
REYNALDO.
Well, my lord.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Farewell.
[Exit Reynaldo.]

Enter Ophelia.

How now, Ophelia, what’s the matter?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
Alas, my lord, I have been so affrighted.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
With what, in the name of God?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
My lord, as I was sewing in my chamber,
Lord Hamlet, with his doublet all unbrac’d,
No hat upon his head, his stockings foul’d,
Ungart’red, and down-gyved to his ankle,
Pale as his shirt, his knees knocking each other,
And with a look so piteous in purport
As if he had been loosed out of hell
To speak of horrors, he comes before me.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Mad for thy love?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
My lord, I do not know, but truly I do fear it.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
What said he?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
He took me by the wrist and held me hard;
Then goes he to the length of all his arm;
And with his other hand thus o’er his brow,
He falls to such perusal of my face
As he would draw it. Long stay’d he so,
At last,—a little shaking of mine arm,
And thrice his head thus waving up and down,
He rais’d a sigh so piteous and profound
As it did seem to shatter all his bulk
And end his being. That done, he lets me go,
And with his head over his shoulder turn’d
He seem’d to find his way without his eyes,
For out o’ doors he went without their help,
And to the last bended their light on me.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Come, go with me. I will go seek the King.
This is the very ecstasy of love,
Whose violent property fordoes itself,
And leads the will to desperate undertakings,
As oft as any passion under heaven
That does afflict our natures. I am sorry,—
What, have you given him any hard words of late?

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
No, my good lord; but as you did command,
I did repel his letters and denied
His access to me.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
That hath made him mad.
I am sorry that with better heed and judgement
I had not quoted him. I fear’d he did but trifle,
And meant to wreck thee. But beshrew my jealousy!
It seems it is as proper to our age
To cast beyond ourselves in our opinions
As it is common for the younger sort
To lack discretion. Come, go we to the King.
This must be known, which, being kept close, might move
More grief to hide than hate to utter love.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE II. A room in the Castle.
entities.content: A room in the Castle.
Enter King, Queen, Rosencrantz, Guildenstern and Attendants.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Welcome, dear Rosencrantz and Guildenstern.
Moreover that we much did long to see you,
The need we have to use you did provoke
Our hasty sending. Something have you heard
Of Hamlet’s transformation; so I call it,
Since nor th’exterior nor the inward man
Resembles that it was. What it should be,
More than his father’s death, that thus hath put him
So much from th’understanding of himself,
I cannot dream of. I entreat you both
That, being of so young days brought up with him,
And since so neighbour’d to his youth and humour,
That you vouchsafe your rest here in our court
Some little time, so by your companies
To draw him on to pleasures and to gather,
So much as from occasion you may glean,
Whether aught to us unknown afflicts him thus
That, open’d, lies within our remedy.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Good gentlemen, he hath much talk’d of you,
And sure I am, two men there are not living
To whom he more adheres. If it will please you
To show us so much gentry and good will
As to expend your time with us awhile,
For the supply and profit of our hope,
Your visitation shall receive such thanks
As fits a king’s remembrance.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Both your majesties
Might, by the sovereign power you have of us,
Put your dread pleasures more into command
Than to entreaty.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
We both obey,
And here give up ourselves, in the full bent,
To lay our service freely at your feet
To be commanded.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Thanks, Rosencrantz and gentle Guildenstern.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Thanks, Guildenstern and gentle Rosencrantz.
And I beseech you instantly to visit
My too much changed son. Go, some of you,
And bring these gentlemen where Hamlet is.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
Heavens make our presence and our practices
Pleasant and helpful to him.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Ay, amen.
[Exeunt Rosencrantz, Guildenstern and some Attendants.]
Enter Polonius.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Th’ambassadors from Norway, my good lord,
Are joyfully return’d.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Thou still hast been the father of good news.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Have I, my lord? Assure you, my good liege,
I hold my duty, as I hold my soul,
Both to my God and to my gracious King:
And I do think,—or else this brain of mine
Hunts not the trail of policy so sure
As it hath us’d to do—that I have found
The very cause of Hamlet’s lunacy.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
O speak of that, that do I long to hear.

entities.title: POLONIUS
KING
entities.content:
KING.
Thyself do grace to them, and bring them in.

[Exit Polonius.]

He tells me, my sweet queen, that he hath found
The head and source of all your son’s distemper.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
I doubt it is no other but the main,
His father’s death and our o’erhasty marriage.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Well, we shall sift him.

Enter Polonius with Voltemand and Cornelius.

Welcome, my good friends!
Say, Voltemand, what from our brother Norway?

entities.title: VOLTEMAND
entities.content:
VOLTEMAND.
Most fair return of greetings and desires.
Upon our first, he sent out to suppress
His nephew’s levies, which to him appear’d
To be a preparation ’gainst the Polack;
But better look’d into, he truly found
It was against your Highness; whereat griev’d,
That so his sickness, age, and impotence
Was falsely borne in hand, sends out arrests
On Fortinbras; which he, in brief, obeys,
Receives rebuke from Norway; and in fine,
Makes vow before his uncle never more
To give th’assay of arms against your Majesty.
Whereon old Norway, overcome with joy,
Gives him three thousand crowns in annual fee,
And his commission to employ those soldiers
So levied as before, against the Polack:
With an entreaty, herein further shown,
[Gives a paper.]
That it might please you to give quiet pass
Through your dominions for this enterprise,
On such regards of safety and allowance
As therein are set down.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
It likes us well;
And at our more consider’d time we’ll read,
Answer, and think upon this business.
Meantime we thank you for your well-took labour.
Go to your rest, at night we’ll feast together:.
Most welcome home.
[Exeunt Voltemand and Cornelius.]

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
This business is well ended.
My liege and madam, to expostulate
What majesty should be, what duty is,
Why day is day, night night, and time is time
Were nothing but to waste night, day and time.
Therefore, since brevity is the soul of wit,
And tediousness the limbs and outward flourishes,
I will be brief. Your noble son is mad.
Mad call I it; for to define true madness,
What is’t but to be nothing else but mad?
But let that go.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
More matter, with less art.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Madam, I swear I use no art at all.
That he is mad, ’tis true: ’tis true ’tis pity;
And pity ’tis ’tis true. A foolish figure,
But farewell it, for I will use no art.
Mad let us grant him then. And now remains
That we find out the cause of this effect,
Or rather say, the cause of this defect,
For this effect defective comes by cause.
Thus it remains, and the remainder thus. Perpend,
I have a daughter—have whilst she is mine—
Who in her duty and obedience, mark,
Hath given me this. Now gather, and surmise.
[Reads.]
To the celestial, and my soul’s idol, the most beautified Ophelia—
That’s an ill phrase, a vile phrase; ‘beautified’ is a vile
phrase: but you shall hear.
[Reads.]
these; in her excellent white bosom, these, &c.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Came this from Hamlet to her?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Good madam, stay awhile; I will be faithful.
[Reads.]
Doubt thou the stars are fire,
Doubt that the sun doth move,
Doubt truth to be a liar,
But never doubt I love.
O dear Ophelia, I am ill at these numbers. I have not art to reckon my groans. But that I love thee best, O most best, believe it. Adieu.
Thine evermore, most dear lady, whilst this machine is to him,
HAMLET.
This in obedience hath my daughter show’d me;
And more above, hath his solicitings,
As they fell out by time, by means, and place,
All given to mine ear.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
But how hath she receiv’d his love?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
What do you think of me?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
As of a man faithful and honourable.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
I would fain prove so. But what might you think,
When I had seen this hot love on the wing,
As I perceiv’d it, I must tell you that,
Before my daughter told me, what might you,
Or my dear Majesty your queen here, think,
If I had play’d the desk or table-book,
Or given my heart a winking, mute and dumb,
Or look’d upon this love with idle sight,
What might you think? No, I went round to work,
And my young mistress thus I did bespeak:
‘Lord Hamlet is a prince, out of thy star.
This must not be.’ And then I precepts gave her,
That she should lock herself from his resort,
Admit no messengers, receive no tokens.
Which done, she took the fruits of my advice,
And he, repulsed,—a short tale to make—
Fell into a sadness, then into a fast,
Thence to a watch, thence into a weakness,
Thence to a lightness, and, by this declension,
Into the madness wherein now he raves,
And all we wail for.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Do you think ’tis this?

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
It may be, very likely.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Hath there been such a time, I’d fain know that,
That I have positively said ‘’Tis so,’
When it prov’d otherwise?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Not that I know.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Take this from this, if this be otherwise.
[Points to his head and shoulder.]
If circumstances lead me, I will find
Where truth is hid, though it were hid indeed
Within the centre.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
How may we try it further?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
You know sometimes he walks four hours together
Here in the lobby.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
So he does indeed.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
At such a time I’ll loose my daughter to him.
Be you and I behind an arras then,
Mark the encounter. If he love her not,
And be not from his reason fall’n thereon,
Let me be no assistant for a state,
But keep a farm and carters.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
We will try it.
Enter Hamlet, reading.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
But look where sadly the poor wretch comes reading.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Away, I do beseech you, both away
I’ll board him presently. O, give me leave.

[Exeunt King, Queen and Attendants.]

How does my good Lord Hamlet?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Well, God-a-mercy.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Do you know me, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Excellent well. You are a fishmonger.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Not I, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then I would you were so honest a man.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Honest, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Ay sir, to be honest, as this world goes, is to be one man picked out of ten thousand.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
That’s very true, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
For if the sun breed maggots in a dead dog, being a good kissing carrion,—
Have you a daughter?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
I have, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Let her not walk i’ th’ sun. Conception is a blessing, but not as your daughter may conceive. Friend, look to’t.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
How say you by that? [Aside.] Still harping on my daughter. Yet he knew me not at first; he said I was a fishmonger. He is far gone, far gone. And truly in my youth I suffered much extremity for love; very near this. I’ll speak to him again.—What do you read, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Words, words, words.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
What is the matter, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Between who?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
I mean the matter that you read, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Slanders, sir. For the satirical slave says here that old men have grey beards; that their faces are wrinkled; their eyes purging thick amber and plum-tree gum; and that they have a plentiful lack of wit, together with most weak hams. All which, sir, though I most powerfully and potently believe, yet I hold it not honesty to have it thus set down. For you yourself, sir, should be old as I am, if like a crab you could go backward.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
[Aside.] Though this be madness, yet there is method in’t.—
Will you walk out of the air, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Into my grave?

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Indeed, that is out o’ the air. [Aside.] How pregnant sometimes his replies are! A happiness that often madness hits on, which reason and sanity could not so prosperously be delivered of. I will leave him and suddenly contrive the means of meeting between him and my daughter.
My honourable lord, I will most humbly take my leave of you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
You cannot, sir, take from me anything that I will more willingly part withal, except my life, except my life, except my life.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Fare you well, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
These tedious old fools.
Enter Rosencrantz and Guildenstern.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
You go to seek the Lord Hamlet; there he is.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
[To Polonius.] God save you, sir.
[Exit Polonius.]

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
My honoured lord!

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
My most dear lord!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
My excellent good friends! How dost thou, Guildenstern? Ah, Rosencrantz. Good lads, how do ye both?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
As the indifferent children of the earth.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Nor the soles of her shoe?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Neither, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then you live about her waist, or in the middle of her favours?

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
Faith, her privates we.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
In the secret parts of Fortune? O, most true; she is a strumpet. What’s the news?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
None, my lord, but that the world’s grown honest.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then is doomsday near. But your news is not true. Let me question more in particular. What have you, my good friends, deserved at the hands of Fortune, that she sends you to prison hither?

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
Prison, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Denmark’s a prison.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Then is the world one.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
A goodly one; in which there are many confines, wards, and dungeons, Denmark being one o’ th’ worst.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
We think not so, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, then ’tis none to you; for there is nothing either good or bad but thinking makes it so. To me it is a prison.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Why, then your ambition makes it one; ’tis too narrow for your mind.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O God, I could be bounded in a nutshell, and count myself a king of infinite space, were it not that I have bad dreams.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
Which dreams, indeed, are ambition; for the very substance of the ambitious is merely the shadow of a dream.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
A dream itself is but a shadow.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Truly, and I hold ambition of so airy and light a quality that it is but a shadow’s shadow.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then are our beggars bodies, and our monarchs and outstretch’d heroes the beggars’ shadows. Shall we to th’ court? For, by my fay, I cannot reason.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ AND GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ and GUILDENSTERN.
We’ll wait upon you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
No such matter. I will not sort you with the rest of my servants; for, to speak to you like an honest man, I am most dreadfully attended. But, in the beaten way of friendship, what make you at Elsinore?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
To visit you, my lord, no other occasion.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Beggar that I am, I am even poor in thanks; but I thank you. And sure, dear friends, my thanks are too dear a halfpenny. Were you not sent for? Is it your own inclining? Is it a free visitation? Come, deal justly with me. Come, come; nay, speak.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
What should we say, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, anything. But to the purpose. You were sent for; and there is a kind of confession in your looks, which your modesties have not craft enough to colour. I know the good King and Queen have sent for you.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
To what end, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
That you must teach me. But let me conjure you, by the rights of our fellowship, by the consonancy of our youth, by the obligation of our ever-preserved love, and by what more dear a better proposer could charge you withal, be even and direct with me, whether you were sent for or no.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
[To Guildenstern.] What say you?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
[Aside.] Nay, then I have an eye of you. If you love me, hold not off.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
My lord, we were sent for.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I will tell you why; so shall my anticipation prevent your discovery, and your secrecy to the King and Queen moult no feather. I have of late, but wherefore I know not, lost all my mirth, forgone all custom of exercises; and indeed, it goes so heavily with my disposition that this goodly frame the earth, seems to me a sterile promontory; this most excellent canopy the air, look you, this brave o’erhanging firmament, this majestical roof fretted with golden fire, why, it appears no other thing to me than a foul and pestilent congregation of vapours. What a piece of work is man, how noble in reason, how infinite in faculties, in form and moving, how express and admirable; in action how like an angel, in apprehension, how like a god: the beauty of the world, the paragon of animals. And yet, to me, what is this quintessence of dust? Man delights not me; no, nor woman neither, though by your smiling you seem to say so.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
My lord, there was no such stuff in my thoughts.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why did you laugh then, when I said ‘Man delights not me’?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
To think, my lord, if you delight not in man, what Lenten entertainment the players shall receive from you. We coted them on the way, and hither are they coming to offer you service.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
He that plays the king shall be welcome,—his Majesty shall have tribute of me; the adventurous knight shall use his foil and target; the lover shall not sigh gratis, the humorous man shall end his part in peace; the clown shall make those laugh whose lungs are tickle o’ th’ sere; and the lady shall say her mind freely, or the blank verse shall halt for’t. What players are they?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Even those you were wont to take such delight in—the tragedians of the city.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How chances it they travel? Their residence, both in reputation and profit, was better both ways.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
I think their inhibition comes by the means of the late innovation.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Do they hold the same estimation they did when I was in the city? Are they so followed?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
No, indeed, they are not.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How comes it? Do they grow rusty?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Nay, their endeavour keeps in the wonted pace; but there is, sir, an aerie of children, little eyases, that cry out on the top of question, and are most tyrannically clapped for’t. These are now the fashion, and so berattle the common stages—so they call them—that many wearing rapiers are afraid of goose-quills and dare scarce come thither.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What, are they children? Who maintains ’em? How are they escoted? Will they pursue the quality no longer than they can sing? Will they not say afterwards, if they should grow themselves to common players—as it is most like, if their means are no better—their writers do them wrong to make them exclaim against their own succession?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Faith, there has been much to do on both sides; and the nation holds it no sin to tarre them to controversy. There was for a while, no money bid for argument unless the poet and the player went to cuffs in the question.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Is’t possible?

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
O, there has been much throwing about of brains.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Do the boys carry it away?

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Ay, that they do, my lord. Hercules and his load too.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
It is not very strange; for my uncle is King of Denmark, and those that would make mouths at him while my father lived, give twenty, forty, fifty, a hundred ducats apiece for his picture in little. ’Sblood, there is something in this more than natural, if philosophy could find it out.
[Flourish of trumpets within.]

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
There are the players.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Gentlemen, you are welcome to Elsinore. Your hands, come. The appurtenance of welcome is fashion and ceremony. Let me comply with you in this garb, lest my extent to the players, which I tell you must show fairly outward, should more appear like entertainment than yours. You are welcome. But my uncle-father and aunt-mother are deceived.

entities.title: GUILDENSTERN
entities.content:
GUILDENSTERN.
In what, my dear lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I am but mad north-north-west. When the wind is southerly, I know a hawk from a handsaw.
Enter Polonius.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Well be with you, gentlemen.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Hark you, Guildenstern, and you too, at each ear a hearer. That great baby you see there is not yet out of his swaddling clouts.

entities.title: ROSENCRANTZ
entities.content:
ROSENCRANTZ.
Happily he’s the second time come to them; for they say an old man is twice a child.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I will prophesy he comes to tell me of the players. Mark it.—You say right, sir: for a Monday morning ’twas so indeed.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
My lord, I have news to tell you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
My lord, I have news to tell you. When Roscius was an actor in Rome—

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
The actors are come hither, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Buzz, buzz.

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
Upon my honour.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Then came each actor on his ass—

entities.title: POLONIUS
entities.content:
POLONIUS.
The best actors in the world, either for tragedy, comedy, history, pastoral, pastoral-comical, historical-pastoral, tragical-historical, tragical-comical-historical-pastoral, scene individable, or poem unlimited.
