Her brother is in secret come from France,
Feeds on his wonder, keeps himself in clouds,
And wants not buzzers to infect his ear
With pestilent speeches of his father’s death,
Wherein necessity, of matter beggar’d,
Will nothing stick our person to arraign
In ear and ear. O my dear Gertrude, this,
Like to a murdering piece, in many places
Gives me superfluous death.
[A noise within.]

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Alack, what noise is this?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Where are my Switzers? Let them guard the door.

Enter a Gentleman.

What is the matter?

entities.title: GENTLEMAN
entities.content:
GENTLEMAN.
Save yourself, my lord.
The ocean, overpeering of his list,
Eats not the flats with more impetuous haste
Than young Laertes, in a riotous head,
O’erbears your offices. The rabble call him lord,
And, as the world were now but to begin,
Antiquity forgot, custom not known,
The ratifiers and props of every word,
They cry ‘Choose we! Laertes shall be king!’
Caps, hands, and tongues applaud it to the clouds,
‘Laertes shall be king, Laertes king.’

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
How cheerfully on the false trail they cry.
O, this is counter, you false Danish dogs.
[A noise within.]

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
The doors are broke.
Enter Laertes, armed; Danes following.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Where is this king?—Sirs, stand you all without.

Danes.
No, let’s come in.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I pray you, give me leave.

entities.title: DANES
entities.content:
DANES.
We will, we will.
[They retire without the door.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I thank you. Keep the door. O thou vile king,
Give me my father.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Calmly, good Laertes.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
That drop of blood that’s calm proclaims me bastard;
Cries cuckold to my father, brands the harlot
Even here between the chaste unsmirched brow
Of my true mother.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
What is the cause, Laertes,
That thy rebellion looks so giant-like?—
Let him go, Gertrude. Do not fear our person.
There’s such divinity doth hedge a king,
That treason can but peep to what it would,
Acts little of his will.—Tell me, Laertes,
Why thou art thus incens’d.—Let him go, Gertrude:—
Speak, man.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Where is my father?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Dead.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
But not by him.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Let him demand his fill.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
How came he dead? I’ll not be juggled with.
To hell, allegiance! Vows, to the blackest devil!
Conscience and grace, to the profoundest pit!
I dare damnation. To this point I stand,
That both the worlds, I give to negligence,
Let come what comes; only I’ll be reveng’d
Most throughly for my father.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Who shall stay you?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
My will, not all the world.
And for my means, I’ll husband them so well,
They shall go far with little.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Good Laertes,
If you desire to know the certainty
Of your dear father’s death, is’t writ in your revenge
That, sweepstake, you will draw both friend and foe,
Winner and loser?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
None but his enemies.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Will you know them then?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
To his good friends thus wide I’ll ope my arms;
And, like the kind life-rendering pelican,
Repast them with my blood.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Why, now you speak
Like a good child and a true gentleman.
That I am guiltless of your father’s death,
And am most sensibly in grief for it,
It shall as level to your judgement ’pear
As day does to your eye.

entities.title: DANES
entities.content:
DANES.
[Within.] Let her come in.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
How now! What noise is that?

Re-enter Ophelia, fantastically dressed with straws and flowers.

O heat, dry up my brains. Tears seven times salt,
Burn out the sense and virtue of mine eye.
By heaven, thy madness shall be paid by weight,
Till our scale turn the beam. O rose of May!
Dear maid, kind sister, sweet Ophelia!
O heavens, is’t possible a young maid’s wits
Should be as mortal as an old man’s life?
Nature is fine in love, and where ’tis fine,
It sends some precious instance of itself
After the thing it loves.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
[Sings.]
They bore him barefac’d on the bier,
Hey non nonny, nonny, hey nonny
And on his grave rain’d many a tear.—
Fare you well, my dove!

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Hadst thou thy wits, and didst persuade revenge,
It could not move thus.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
You must sing ‘Down a-down, and you call him a-down-a.’ O, how the wheel becomes it! It is the false steward that stole his master’s daughter.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
This nothing’s more than matter.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
There’s rosemary, that’s for remembrance; pray love, remember. And there is pansies, that’s for thoughts.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
A document in madness, thoughts and remembrance fitted.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
There’s fennel for you, and columbines. There’s rue for you; and here’s some for me. We may call it herb of grace o’ Sundays. O you must wear your rue with a difference. There’s a daisy. I would give you some violets, but they wither’d all when my father died. They say he made a good end.
[Sings.]
For bonny sweet Robin is all my joy.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Thought and affliction, passion, hell itself
She turns to favour and to prettiness.

entities.title: OPHELIA
entities.content:
OPHELIA.
[Sings.]
And will he not come again?
And will he not come again?
No, no, he is dead,
Go to thy death-bed,
He never will come again.

His beard was as white as snow,
All flaxen was his poll.
He is gone, he is gone,
And we cast away moan.
God ha’ mercy on his soul.

And of all Christian souls, I pray God. God b’ wi’ ye.
[Exit.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Do you see this, O God?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Laertes, I must commune with your grief,
Or you deny me right. Go but apart,
Make choice of whom your wisest friends you will,
And they shall hear and judge ’twixt you and me.
If by direct or by collateral hand
They find us touch’d, we will our kingdom give,
Our crown, our life, and all that we call ours
To you in satisfaction; but if not,
Be you content to lend your patience to us,
And we shall jointly labour with your soul
To give it due content.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Let this be so;
His means of death, his obscure burial,—
No trophy, sword, nor hatchment o’er his bones,
No noble rite, nor formal ostentation,—
Cry to be heard, as ’twere from heaven to earth,
That I must call’t in question.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
So you shall.
And where th’offence is let the great axe fall.
I pray you go with me.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE VI. Another room in the Castle.
entities.content: Another room in the Castle.
Enter Horatio and a Servant.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What are they that would speak with me?

entities.title: SERVANT
entities.content:
SERVANT.
Sailors, sir. They say they have letters for you.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Let them come in.

[Exit Servant.]

I do not know from what part of the world
I should be greeted, if not from Lord Hamlet.
Enter Sailors.

entities.title: FIRST SAILOR
entities.content:
FIRST SAILOR.
God bless you, sir.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Let him bless thee too.

entities.title: FIRST SAILOR
entities.content:
FIRST SAILOR.
He shall, sir, and’t please him. There’s a letter for you, sir. It comes from th’ambassador that was bound for England; if your name be Horatio, as I am let to know it is.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
[Reads.] ‘Horatio, when thou shalt have overlooked this, give these fellows some means to the King. They have letters for him. Ere we were two days old at sea, a pirate of very warlike appointment gave us chase. Finding ourselves too slow of sail, we put on a compelled valour, and in the grapple I boarded them. On the instant they got clear of our ship, so I alone became their prisoner. They have dealt with me like thieves of mercy. But they knew what they did; I am to do a good turn for them. Let the King have the letters I have sent, and repair thou to me with as much haste as thou wouldst fly death. I have words to speak in thine ear will make thee dumb; yet are they much too light for the bore of the matter. These good fellows will bring thee where I am. Rosencrantz and Guildenstern hold their course for England: of them I have much to tell thee. Farewell.
He that thou knowest thine,
HAMLET.’

Come, I will give you way for these your letters,
And do’t the speedier, that you may direct me
To him from whom you brought them.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE VII. Another room in the Castle.
entities.content: Another room in the Castle.
Enter King and Laertes.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Now must your conscience my acquittance seal,
And you must put me in your heart for friend,
Sith you have heard, and with a knowing ear,
That he which hath your noble father slain
Pursu’d my life.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
It well appears. But tell me
Why you proceeded not against these feats,
So crimeful and so capital in nature,
As by your safety, wisdom, all things else,
You mainly were stirr’d up.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
O, for two special reasons,
Which may to you, perhaps, seem much unsinew’d,
But yet to me they are strong. The Queen his mother
Lives almost by his looks; and for myself,—
My virtue or my plague, be it either which,—
She’s so conjunctive to my life and soul,
That, as the star moves not but in his sphere,
I could not but by her. The other motive,
Why to a public count I might not go,
Is the great love the general gender bear him,
Who, dipping all his faults in their affection,
Would like the spring that turneth wood to stone,
Convert his gyves to graces; so that my arrows,
Too slightly timber’d for so loud a wind,
Would have reverted to my bow again,
And not where I had aim’d them.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
And so have I a noble father lost,
A sister driven into desperate terms,
Whose worth, if praises may go back again,
Stood challenger on mount of all the age
For her perfections. But my revenge will come.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Break not your sleeps for that. You must not think
That we are made of stuff so flat and dull
That we can let our beard be shook with danger,
And think it pastime. You shortly shall hear more.
I lov’d your father, and we love ourself,
And that, I hope, will teach you to imagine—

Enter a Messenger.

How now? What news?

entities.title: MESSENGER
entities.content:
MESSENGER.
Letters, my lord, from Hamlet.
This to your Majesty; this to the Queen.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
From Hamlet! Who brought them?

entities.title: MESSENGER
entities.content:
MESSENGER.
Sailors, my lord, they say; I saw them not.
They were given me by Claudio. He receiv’d them
Of him that brought them.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Laertes, you shall hear them.
Leave us.

[Exit Messenger.]

[Reads.] ‘High and mighty, you shall know I am set naked on your kingdom. Tomorrow shall I beg leave to see your kingly eyes. When I shall, first asking your pardon thereunto, recount the occasions of my sudden and more strange return.
HAMLET.’

What should this mean? Are all the rest come back?
Or is it some abuse, and no such thing?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Know you the hand?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
’Tis Hamlet’s character. ‘Naked!’
And in a postscript here he says ‘alone.’
Can you advise me?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I am lost in it, my lord. But let him come,
It warms the very sickness in my heart
That I shall live and tell him to his teeth,
‘Thus diest thou.’

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
If it be so, Laertes,—
As how should it be so? How otherwise?—
Will you be rul’d by me?

entities.title: LAERTES
KING
entities.content:
KING.
To thine own peace. If he be now return’d,
As checking at his voyage, and that he means
No more to undertake it, I will work him
To an exploit, now ripe in my device,
Under the which he shall not choose but fall;
And for his death no wind shall breathe,
But even his mother shall uncharge the practice
And call it accident.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
My lord, I will be rul’d;
The rather if you could devise it so
That I might be the organ.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
It falls right.
You have been talk’d of since your travel much,
And that in Hamlet’s hearing, for a quality
Wherein they say you shine. Your sum of parts
Did not together pluck such envy from him
As did that one, and that, in my regard,
Of the unworthiest siege.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
What part is that, my lord?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
A very riband in the cap of youth,
Yet needful too, for youth no less becomes
The light and careless livery that it wears
Than settled age his sables and his weeds,
Importing health and graveness. Two months since
Here was a gentleman of Normandy,—
I’ve seen myself, and serv’d against, the French,
And they can well on horseback, but this gallant
Had witchcraft in’t. He grew unto his seat,
And to such wondrous doing brought his horse,
As had he been incorps’d and demi-natur’d
With the brave beast. So far he topp’d my thought
That I in forgery of shapes and tricks,
Come short of what he did.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
A Norman was’t?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
A Norman.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Upon my life, Lamord.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
The very same.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I know him well. He is the brooch indeed
And gem of all the nation.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
He made confession of you,
And gave you such a masterly report
For art and exercise in your defence,
And for your rapier most especially,
That he cried out ’twould be a sight indeed
If one could match you. The scrimers of their nation
He swore had neither motion, guard, nor eye,
If you oppos’d them. Sir, this report of his
Did Hamlet so envenom with his envy
That he could nothing do but wish and beg
Your sudden coming o’er to play with him.
Now, out of this,—

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
What out of this, my lord?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Laertes, was your father dear to you?
Or are you like the painting of a sorrow,
A face without a heart?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Why ask you this?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Not that I think you did not love your father,
But that I know love is begun by time,
And that I see, in passages of proof,
Time qualifies the spark and fire of it.
There lives within the very flame of love
A kind of wick or snuff that will abate it;
And nothing is at a like goodness still,
For goodness, growing to a pleurisy,
Dies in his own too much. That we would do,
We should do when we would; for this ‘would’ changes,
And hath abatements and delays as many
As there are tongues, are hands, are accidents;
And then this ‘should’ is like a spendthrift sigh
That hurts by easing. But to the quick o’ th’ulcer:
Hamlet comes back: what would you undertake
To show yourself your father’s son in deed,
More than in words?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
To cut his throat i’ th’ church.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
No place, indeed, should murder sanctuarize;
Revenge should have no bounds. But good Laertes,
Will you do this, keep close within your chamber.
Hamlet return’d shall know you are come home:
We’ll put on those shall praise your excellence,
And set a double varnish on the fame
The Frenchman gave you, bring you in fine together
And wager on your heads. He, being remiss,
Most generous, and free from all contriving,
Will not peruse the foils; so that with ease,
Or with a little shuffling, you may choose
A sword unbated, and in a pass of practice,
Requite him for your father.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I will do’t.
And for that purpose I’ll anoint my sword.
I bought an unction of a mountebank
So mortal that, but dip a knife in it,
Where it draws blood no cataplasm so rare,
Collected from all simples that have virtue
Under the moon, can save the thing from death
This is but scratch’d withal. I’ll touch my point
With this contagion, that if I gall him slightly,
It may be death.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Let’s further think of this,
Weigh what convenience both of time and means
May fit us to our shape. If this should fail,
And that our drift look through our bad performance.
’Twere better not assay’d. Therefore this project
Should have a back or second, that might hold
If this did blast in proof. Soft, let me see.
We’ll make a solemn wager on your cunnings,—
I ha’t! When in your motion you are hot and dry,
As make your bouts more violent to that end,
And that he calls for drink, I’ll have prepar’d him
A chalice for the nonce; whereon but sipping,
If he by chance escape your venom’d stuck,
Our purpose may hold there.

Enter Queen.

How now, sweet Queen?

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
One woe doth tread upon another’s heel,
So fast they follow. Your sister’s drown’d, Laertes.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Drown’d! O, where?

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
There is a willow grows aslant a brook,
That shows his hoary leaves in the glassy stream.
There with fantastic garlands did she make
Of crow-flowers, nettles, daisies, and long purples,
That liberal shepherds give a grosser name,
But our cold maids do dead men’s fingers call them.
There on the pendant boughs her coronet weeds
Clamb’ring to hang, an envious sliver broke,
When down her weedy trophies and herself
Fell in the weeping brook. Her clothes spread wide,
And mermaid-like, awhile they bore her up,
Which time she chaunted snatches of old tunes,
As one incapable of her own distress,
Or like a creature native and indued
Unto that element. But long it could not be
Till that her garments, heavy with their drink,
Pull’d the poor wretch from her melodious lay
To muddy death.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Alas, then she is drown’d?

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Drown’d, drown’d.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Too much of water hast thou, poor Ophelia,
And therefore I forbid my tears. But yet
It is our trick; nature her custom holds,
Let shame say what it will. When these are gone,
The woman will be out. Adieu, my lord,
I have a speech of fire, that fain would blaze,
But that this folly douts it.
[Exit.]

entities.title: KING

entities.content: [Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE I. A churchyard.
entities.content: A churchyard.
Enter two Clowns with spades, &c.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Is she to be buried in Christian burial, when she wilfully seeks her own salvation?

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
I tell thee she is, and therefore make her grave straight. The crowner hath sat on her, and finds it Christian burial.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
How can that be, unless she drowned herself in her own defence?

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Why, ’tis found so.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
It must be se offendendo, it cannot be else. For here lies the point: if I drown myself wittingly, it argues an act: and an act hath three branches. It is to act, to do, and to perform: argal, she drowned herself wittingly.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Nay, but hear you, goodman delver,—

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Give me leave. Here lies the water; good. Here stands the man; good. If the man go to this water and drown himself, it is, will he nill he, he goes,—mark you that. But if the water come to him and drown him, he drowns not himself. Argal, he that is not guilty of his own death shortens not his own life.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
But is this law?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Ay, marry, is’t, crowner’s quest law.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Will you ha’ the truth on’t? If this had not been a gentlewoman, she should have been buried out o’ Christian burial.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Why, there thou say’st. And the more pity that great folk should have countenance in this world to drown or hang themselves more than their even Christian. Come, my spade. There is no ancient gentlemen but gardeners, ditchers, and grave-makers: they hold up Adam’s profession.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Was he a gentleman?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
He was the first that ever bore arms.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Why, he had none.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
What, art a heathen? How dost thou understand the Scripture? The Scripture says Adam digg’d. Could he dig without arms? I’ll put another question to thee. If thou answerest me not to the purpose, confess thyself—

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Go to.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
What is he that builds stronger than either the mason, the shipwright, or the carpenter?

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
The gallows-maker; for that frame outlives a thousand tenants.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
I like thy wit well in good faith, the gallows does well. But how does it well? It does well to those that do ill. Now, thou dost ill to say the gallows is built stronger than the church; argal, the gallows may do well to thee. To’t again, come.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Who builds stronger than a mason, a shipwright, or a carpenter?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Ay, tell me that, and unyoke.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Marry, now I can tell.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
To’t.

entities.title: SECOND CLOWN
entities.content:
SECOND CLOWN.
Mass, I cannot tell.
Enter Hamlet and Horatio, at a distance.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Cudgel thy brains no more about it, for your dull ass will not mend his pace with beating; and when you are asked this question next, say ‘a grave-maker’. The houses he makes last till doomsday. Go, get thee to Yaughan; fetch me a stoup of liquor.

[Exit Second Clown.]

[Digs and sings.]

In youth when I did love, did love,
Methought it was very sweet;
To contract, O, the time for, a, my behove,
O methought there was nothing meet.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Has this fellow no feeling of his business, that he sings at grave-making?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Custom hath made it in him a property of easiness.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
’Tis e’en so; the hand of little employment hath the daintier sense.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
[Sings.]
But age with his stealing steps
Hath claw’d me in his clutch,
And hath shipp’d me into the land,
As if I had never been such.
[Throws up a skull.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
That skull had a tongue in it, and could sing once. How the knave jowls it to th’ ground, as if ’twere Cain’s jawbone, that did the first murder! This might be the pate of a politician which this ass now o’er-offices, one that would circumvent God, might it not?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It might, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Or of a courtier, which could say ‘Good morrow, sweet lord! How dost thou, good lord?’ This might be my lord such-a-one, that praised my lord such-a-one’s horse when he meant to beg it, might it not?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Ay, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, e’en so: and now my Lady Worm’s; chapless, and knocked about the mazard with a sexton’s spade. Here’s fine revolution, an we had the trick to see’t. Did these bones cost no more the breeding but to play at loggets with ’em? Mine ache to think on’t.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
[Sings.]
A pickaxe and a spade, a spade,
For and a shrouding-sheet;
O, a pit of clay for to be made
For such a guest is meet.
[Throws up another skull.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
There’s another. Why may not that be the skull of a lawyer? Where be his quiddits now, his quillets, his cases, his tenures, and his tricks? Why does he suffer this rude knave now to knock him about the sconce with a dirty shovel, and will not tell him of his action of battery? Hum. This fellow might be in’s time a great buyer of land, with his statutes, his recognizances, his fines, his double vouchers, his recoveries. Is this the fine of his fines, and the recovery of his recoveries, to have his fine pate full of fine dirt? Will his vouchers vouch him no more of his purchases, and double ones too, than the length and breadth of a pair of indentures? The very conveyances of his lands will scarcely lie in this box; and must the inheritor himself have no more, ha?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Not a jot more, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Is not parchment made of sheep-skins?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Ay, my lord, and of calf-skins too.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
They are sheep and calves which seek out assurance in that. I will speak to this fellow.—Whose grave’s this, sir?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Mine, sir.
[Sings.]
O, a pit of clay for to be made
For such a guest is meet.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I think it be thine indeed, for thou liest in’t.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
You lie out on’t, sir, and therefore ’tis not yours.
For my part, I do not lie in’t, yet it is mine.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Thou dost lie in’t, to be in’t and say it is thine. ’Tis for the dead, not for the quick; therefore thou liest.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
’Tis a quick lie, sir; ’t will away again from me to you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What man dost thou dig it for?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
For no man, sir.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What woman then?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
For none neither.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Who is to be buried in’t?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
One that was a woman, sir; but, rest her soul, she’s dead.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How absolute the knave is! We must speak by the card, or equivocation will undo us. By the Lord, Horatio, these three years I have taken note of it, the age is grown so picked that the toe of the peasant comes so near the heel of the courtier he galls his kibe.—How long hast thou been a grave-maker?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Of all the days i’ th’ year, I came to’t that day that our last King Hamlet o’ercame Fortinbras.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How long is that since?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Cannot you tell that? Every fool can tell that. It was the very day that young Hamlet was born,—he that is mad, and sent into England.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Ay, marry, why was he sent into England?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Why, because he was mad; he shall recover his wits there; or if he do not, it’s no great matter there.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
’Twill not be seen in him there; there the men are as mad as he.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How came he mad?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Very strangely, they say.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How strangely?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Faith, e’en with losing his wits.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Upon what ground?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Why, here in Denmark. I have been sexton here, man and boy, thirty years.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How long will a man lie i’ th’earth ere he rot?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Faith, if he be not rotten before he die,—as we have many pocky corses nowadays that will scarce hold the laying in,—he will last you some eight year or nine year. A tanner will last you nine year.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why he more than another?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
Why, sir, his hide is so tann’d with his trade that he will keep out water a great while. And your water is a sore decayer of your whoreson dead body. Here’s a skull now; this skull hath lain in the earth three-and-twenty years.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Whose was it?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
A whoreson, mad fellow’s it was. Whose do you think it was?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Nay, I know not.

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
A pestilence on him for a mad rogue! A pour’d a flagon of Rhenish on my head once. This same skull, sir, was Yorick’s skull, the King’s jester.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
This?

entities.title: FIRST CLOWN
entities.content:
FIRST CLOWN.
E’en that.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Let me see. [Takes the skull.] Alas, poor Yorick. I knew him, Horatio, a fellow of infinite jest, of most excellent fancy. He hath borne me on his back a thousand times; and now, how abhorred in my imagination it is! My gorge rises at it. Here hung those lips that I have kiss’d I know not how oft. Where be your gibes now? your gambols? your songs? your flashes of merriment, that were wont to set the table on a roar? Not one now, to mock your own grinning? Quite chop-fallen? Now get you to my lady’s chamber, and tell her, let her paint an inch thick, to this favour she must come. Make her laugh at that.—Prithee, Horatio, tell me one thing.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What’s that, my lord?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Dost thou think Alexander looked o’ this fashion i’ th’earth?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
E’en so.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
And smelt so? Pah!
[Throws down the skull.]

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
E’en so, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
To what base uses we may return, Horatio! Why may not imagination trace the noble dust of Alexander till he find it stopping a bung-hole?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
’Twere to consider too curiously to consider so.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
No, faith, not a jot. But to follow him thither with modesty enough, and likelihood to lead it; as thus. Alexander died, Alexander was buried, Alexander returneth into dust; the dust is earth; of earth we make loam; and why of that loam whereto he was converted might they not stop a beer-barrel?
Imperious Caesar, dead and turn’d to clay,
Might stop a hole to keep the wind away.
O, that that earth which kept the world in awe
Should patch a wall t’expel the winter’s flaw.
But soft! but soft! aside! Here comes the King.

Enter priests, &c, in procession; the corpse of Ophelia, Laertes and Mourners following; King, Queen, their Trains, &c.

The Queen, the courtiers. Who is that they follow?
And with such maimed rites? This doth betoken
The corse they follow did with desperate hand
Fordo it own life. ’Twas of some estate.
Couch we awhile and mark.
[Retiring with Horatio.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
What ceremony else?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
That is Laertes, a very noble youth. Mark.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
What ceremony else?

entities.title: PRIEST
entities.content:
PRIEST.
Her obsequies have been as far enlarg’d
As we have warranties. Her death was doubtful;
And but that great command o’ersways the order,
She should in ground unsanctified have lodg’d
Till the last trumpet. For charitable prayers,
Shards, flints, and pebbles should be thrown on her.
Yet here she is allowed her virgin rites,
Her maiden strewments, and the bringing home
Of bell and burial.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Must there no more be done?

entities.title: PRIEST
entities.content:
PRIEST.
No more be done.
We should profane the service of the dead
To sing sage requiem and such rest to her
As to peace-parted souls.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Lay her i’ th’earth,
And from her fair and unpolluted flesh
May violets spring. I tell thee, churlish priest,
A minist’ring angel shall my sister be
When thou liest howling.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What, the fair Ophelia?

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
[Scattering flowers.] Sweets to the sweet. Farewell.
I hop’d thou shouldst have been my Hamlet’s wife;
I thought thy bride-bed to have deck’d, sweet maid,
And not have strew’d thy grave.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
O, treble woe
Fall ten times treble on that cursed head
Whose wicked deed thy most ingenious sense
Depriv’d thee of. Hold off the earth a while,
Till I have caught her once more in mine arms.
[Leaps into the grave.]
Now pile your dust upon the quick and dead,
Till of this flat a mountain you have made,
To o’ertop old Pelion or the skyish head
Of blue Olympus.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
[Advancing.]
What is he whose grief
Bears such an emphasis? whose phrase of sorrow
Conjures the wand’ring stars, and makes them stand
Like wonder-wounded hearers? This is I,
Hamlet the Dane.
[Leaps into the grave.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
[Grappling with him.] The devil take thy soul!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Thou pray’st not well.
I prithee take thy fingers from my throat;
For though I am not splenative and rash,
Yet have I in me something dangerous,
Which let thy wiseness fear. Away thy hand!

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Pluck them asunder.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Hamlet! Hamlet!

All.
Gentlemen!

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Good my lord, be quiet.
[The Attendants part them, and they come out of the grave.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, I will fight with him upon this theme
Until my eyelids will no longer wag.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
O my son, what theme?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I lov’d Ophelia; forty thousand brothers
Could not, with all their quantity of love,
Make up my sum. What wilt thou do for her?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
O, he is mad, Laertes.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
For love of God forbear him!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
’Swounds, show me what thou’lt do:
Woul’t weep? woul’t fight? woul’t fast? woul’t tear thyself?
Woul’t drink up eisel? eat a crocodile?
I’ll do’t. Dost thou come here to whine?
To outface me with leaping in her grave?
Be buried quick with her, and so will I.
And if thou prate of mountains, let them throw
Millions of acres on us, till our ground,
Singeing his pate against the burning zone,
Make Ossa like a wart. Nay, an thou’lt mouth,
I’ll rant as well as thou.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
This is mere madness:
And thus awhile the fit will work on him;
Anon, as patient as the female dove,
When that her golden couplets are disclos’d,
His silence will sit drooping.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Hear you, sir;
What is the reason that you use me thus?
I lov’d you ever. But it is no matter.
Let Hercules himself do what he may,
The cat will mew, and dog will have his day.
[Exit.]

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
I pray thee, good Horatio, wait upon him.

[Exit Horatio.]

[To Laertes]
Strengthen your patience in our last night’s speech;
We’ll put the matter to the present push.—
Good Gertrude, set some watch over your son.
This grave shall have a living monument.
An hour of quiet shortly shall we see;
Till then in patience our proceeding be.
[Exeunt.]

entities.title: SCENE II. A hall in the Castle.
entities.content: A hall in the Castle.
Enter Hamlet and Horatio.

entities.title: HAMLET
HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Remember it, my lord!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Sir, in my heart there was a kind of fighting
That would not let me sleep. Methought I lay
Worse than the mutinies in the bilboes. Rashly,
And prais’d be rashness for it,—let us know,
Our indiscretion sometime serves us well,
When our deep plots do pall; and that should teach us
There’s a divinity that shapes our ends,
Rough-hew them how we will.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
That is most certain.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Up from my cabin,
My sea-gown scarf’d about me, in the dark
Grop’d I to find out them; had my desire,
Finger’d their packet, and in fine, withdrew
To mine own room again, making so bold,
My fears forgetting manners, to unseal
Their grand commission; where I found, Horatio,
Oh royal knavery! an exact command,
Larded with many several sorts of reasons,
Importing Denmark’s health, and England’s too,
With ho! such bugs and goblins in my life,
That on the supervise, no leisure bated,
No, not to stay the grinding of the axe,
My head should be struck off.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Is’t possible?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Here’s the commission, read it at more leisure.
But wilt thou hear me how I did proceed?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
I beseech you.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Being thus benetted round with villanies,—
Or I could make a prologue to my brains,
They had begun the play,—I sat me down,
Devis’d a new commission, wrote it fair:
I once did hold it, as our statists do,
A baseness to write fair, and labour’d much
How to forget that learning; but, sir, now
It did me yeoman’s service. Wilt thou know
The effect of what I wrote?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Ay, good my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
An earnest conjuration from the King,
As England was his faithful tributary,
As love between them like the palm might flourish,
As peace should still her wheaten garland wear
And stand a comma ’tween their amities,
And many such-like ‘as’es of great charge,
That on the view and know of these contents,
Without debatement further, more or less,
He should the bearers put to sudden death,
Not shriving-time allow’d.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
How was this seal’d?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, even in that was heaven ordinant.
I had my father’s signet in my purse,
Which was the model of that Danish seal:
Folded the writ up in the form of the other,
Subscrib’d it: gave’t th’impression; plac’d it safely,
The changeling never known. Now, the next day
Was our sea-fight, and what to this was sequent
Thou know’st already.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
So Guildenstern and Rosencrantz go to’t.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Why, man, they did make love to this employment.
They are not near my conscience; their defeat
Does by their own insinuation grow.
’Tis dangerous when the baser nature comes
Between the pass and fell incensed points
Of mighty opposites.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Why, what a king is this!

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Does it not, thinks’t thee, stand me now upon,—
He that hath kill’d my king, and whor’d my mother,
Popp’d in between th’election and my hopes,
Thrown out his angle for my proper life,
And with such cozenage—is’t not perfect conscience
To quit him with this arm? And is’t not to be damn’d
To let this canker of our nature come
In further evil?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
It must be shortly known to him from England
What is the issue of the business there.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
It will be short. The interim is mine;
And a man’s life’s no more than to say ‘One’.
But I am very sorry, good Horatio,
That to Laertes I forgot myself;
For by the image of my cause I see
The portraiture of his. I’ll court his favours.
But sure the bravery of his grief did put me
Into a tow’ring passion.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Peace, who comes here?
Enter Osric.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Your lordship is right welcome back to Denmark.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I humbly thank you, sir. Dost know this waterfly?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
No, my good lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Thy state is the more gracious; for ’tis a vice to know him. He hath much land, and fertile; let a beast be lord of beasts, and his crib shall stand at the king’s mess; ’tis a chough; but, as I say, spacious in the possession of dirt.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Sweet lord, if your lordship were at leisure, I should impart a thing to you from his Majesty.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I will receive it with all diligence of spirit. Put your bonnet to his right use; ’tis for the head.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
I thank your lordship, ’tis very hot.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
No, believe me, ’tis very cold, the wind is northerly.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
It is indifferent cold, my lord, indeed.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Methinks it is very sultry and hot for my complexion.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Exceedingly, my lord; it is very sultry,—as ’twere—I cannot tell how. But, my lord, his Majesty bade me signify to you that he has laid a great wager on your head. Sir, this is the matter,—

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I beseech you, remember,—
[Hamlet moves him to put on his hat.]

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Nay, in good faith; for mine ease, in good faith. Sir, here is newly come to court Laertes; believe me, an absolute gentleman, full of most excellent differences, of very soft society and great showing. Indeed, to speak feelingly of him, he is the card or calendar of gentry; for you shall find in him the continent of what part a gentleman would see.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Sir, his definement suffers no perdition in you, though I know, to divide him inventorially would dizzy th’arithmetic of memory, and yet but yaw neither, in respect of his quick sail. But, in the verity of extolment, I take him to be a soul of great article and his infusion of such dearth and rareness as, to make true diction of him, his semblable is his mirror and who else would trace him his umbrage, nothing more.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Your lordship speaks most infallibly of him.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The concernancy, sir? Why do we wrap the gentleman in our more rawer breath?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Sir?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Is’t not possible to understand in another tongue? You will do’t, sir, really.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What imports the nomination of this gentleman?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Of Laertes?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
His purse is empty already, all’s golden words are spent.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Of him, sir.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
I know you are not ignorant,—

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I would you did, sir; yet in faith if you did, it would not much approve me. Well, sir?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
You are not ignorant of what excellence Laertes is,—

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I dare not confess that, lest I should compare with him in excellence; but to know a man well were to know himself.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
I mean, sir, for his weapon; but in the imputation laid on him, by them in his meed he’s unfellowed.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What’s his weapon?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Rapier and dagger.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
That’s two of his weapons. But well.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
The King, sir, hath wager’d with him six Barbary horses, against the which he has imponed, as I take it, six French rapiers and poniards, with their assigns, as girdle, hangers, and so. Three of the carriages, in faith, are very dear to fancy, very responsive to the hilts, most delicate carriages, and of very liberal conceit.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
What call you the carriages?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
I knew you must be edified by the margin ere you had done.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
The carriages, sir, are the hangers.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The phrase would be more german to the matter if we could carry cannon by our sides. I would it might be hangers till then. But on. Six Barbary horses against six French swords, their assigns, and three liberal conceited carriages: that’s the French bet against the Danish. Why is this all imponed, as you call it?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
The King, sir, hath laid that in a dozen passes between you and him, he shall not exceed you three hits. He hath laid on twelve for nine. And it would come to immediate trial if your lordship would vouchsafe the answer.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How if I answer no?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
I mean, my lord, the opposition of your person in trial.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Sir, I will walk here in the hall. If it please his Majesty, it is the breathing time of day with me. Let the foils be brought, the gentleman willing, and the King hold his purpose, I will win for him if I can; if not, I will gain nothing but my shame and the odd hits.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Shall I re-deliver you e’en so?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
To this effect, sir; after what flourish your nature will.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
I commend my duty to your lordship.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Yours, yours.

[Exit Osric.]

He does well to commend it himself, there are no tongues else for’s turn.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
This lapwing runs away with the shell on his head.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
He did comply with his dug before he suck’d it. Thus has he,—and many more of the same bevy that I know the drossy age dotes on,— only got the tune of the time and outward habit of encounter; a kind of yeasty collection, which carries them through and through the most fanned and winnowed opinions; and do but blow them to their trial, the bubbles are out.
Enter a Lord.

entities.title: LORD
entities.content:
LORD.
My lord, his Majesty commended him to you by young Osric, who brings back to him that you attend him in the hall. He sends to know if your pleasure hold to play with Laertes or that you will take longer time.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I am constant to my purposes, they follow the King’s pleasure. If his fitness speaks, mine is ready. Now or whensoever, provided I be so able as now.

entities.title: LORD
entities.content:
LORD.
The King and Queen and all are coming down.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
In happy time.

entities.title: LORD
entities.content:
LORD.
The Queen desires you to use some gentle entertainment to Laertes before you fall to play.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
She well instructs me.
[Exit Lord.]

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
You will lose this wager, my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I do not think so. Since he went into France, I have been in continual practice. I shall win at the odds. But thou wouldst not think how ill all’s here about my heart: but it is no matter.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Nay, good my lord.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
It is but foolery; but it is such a kind of gain-giving as would perhaps trouble a woman.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
If your mind dislike anything, obey it. I will forestall their repair hither, and say you are not fit.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Not a whit, we defy augury. There’s a special providence in the fall of a sparrow. If it be now, ’tis not to come; if it be not to come, it will be now; if it be not now, yet it will come. The readiness is all. Since no man has aught of what he leaves, what is’t to leave betimes?
Enter King, Queen, Laertes, Lords, Osric and Attendants with foils &c.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Come, Hamlet, come, and take this hand from me.
[The King puts Laertes’s hand into Hamlet’s.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Give me your pardon, sir. I have done you wrong;
But pardon’t as you are a gentleman.
This presence knows, and you must needs have heard,
How I am punish’d with sore distraction.
What I have done
That might your nature, honour, and exception
Roughly awake, I here proclaim was madness.
Was’t Hamlet wrong’d Laertes? Never Hamlet.
If Hamlet from himself be ta’en away,
And when he’s not himself does wrong Laertes,
Then Hamlet does it not, Hamlet denies it.
Who does it, then? His madness. If’t be so,
Hamlet is of the faction that is wrong’d;
His madness is poor Hamlet’s enemy.
Sir, in this audience,
Let my disclaiming from a purpos’d evil
Free me so far in your most generous thoughts
That I have shot my arrow o’er the house
And hurt my brother.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
I am satisfied in nature,
Whose motive in this case should stir me most
To my revenge. But in my terms of honour
I stand aloof, and will no reconcilement
Till by some elder masters of known honour
I have a voice and precedent of peace
To keep my name ungor’d. But till that time
I do receive your offer’d love like love,
And will not wrong it.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I embrace it freely,
And will this brother’s wager frankly play.—
Give us the foils; come on.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Come, one for me.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I’ll be your foil, Laertes; in mine ignorance
Your skill shall like a star i’ th’ darkest night,
Stick fiery off indeed.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
You mock me, sir.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
No, by this hand.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Give them the foils, young Osric. Cousin Hamlet,
You know the wager?

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Very well, my lord.
Your Grace has laid the odds o’ the weaker side.

entities.title: KING
LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
This is too heavy. Let me see another.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
This likes me well. These foils have all a length?
[They prepare to play.]

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Ay, my good lord.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Set me the stoups of wine upon that table.
If Hamlet give the first or second hit,
Or quit in answer of the third exchange,
Let all the battlements their ordnance fire;
The King shall drink to Hamlet’s better breath,
And in the cup an union shall he throw
Richer than that which four successive kings
In Denmark’s crown have worn. Give me the cups;
And let the kettle to the trumpet speak,
The trumpet to the cannoneer without,
The cannons to the heavens, the heavens to earth,
‘Now the King drinks to Hamlet.’ Come, begin.
And you, the judges, bear a wary eye.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Come on, sir.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Come, my lord.
[They play.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
One.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
No.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Judgement.

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
A hit, a very palpable hit.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Well; again.

entities.title: KING

entities.content:
[Trumpets sound, and cannon shot off within.]

Give him the cup.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I’ll play this bout first; set it by awhile.

[They play.]

Come. Another hit; what say you?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
A touch, a touch, I do confess.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Our son shall win.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
He’s fat, and scant of breath.
Here, Hamlet, take my napkin, rub thy brows.
The Queen carouses to thy fortune, Hamlet.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Good madam.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Gertrude, do not drink.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
I will, my lord; I pray you pardon me.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
[Aside.] It is the poison’d cup; it is too late.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
I dare not drink yet, madam. By and by.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
Come, let me wipe thy face.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
My lord, I’ll hit him now.

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
I do not think’t.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
[Aside.] And yet ’tis almost ’gainst my conscience.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Come for the third, Laertes. You do but dally.
I pray you pass with your best violence.
I am afeard you make a wanton of me.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Say you so? Come on.
[They play.]

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Nothing neither way.

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Have at you now.
[Laertes wounds Hamlet; then, in scuffling, they change rapiers, and Hamlet wounds Laertes.]

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
Part them; they are incens’d.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Nay, come again!
[The Queen falls.]

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Look to the Queen there, ho!

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
They bleed on both sides. How is it, my lord?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
How is’t, Laertes?

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
Why, as a woodcock to my own springe, Osric.
I am justly kill’d with mine own treachery.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
How does the Queen?

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
She swoons to see them bleed.

entities.title: QUEEN
entities.content:
QUEEN.
No, no, the drink, the drink! O my dear Hamlet!
The drink, the drink! I am poison’d.
[Dies.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O villany! Ho! Let the door be lock’d:
Treachery! Seek it out.
[Laertes falls.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
It is here, Hamlet. Hamlet, thou art slain.
No medicine in the world can do thee good.
In thee there is not half an hour of life;
The treacherous instrument is in thy hand,
Unbated and envenom’d. The foul practice
Hath turn’d itself on me. Lo, here I lie,
Never to rise again. Thy mother’s poison’d.
I can no more. The King, the King’s to blame.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
The point envenom’d too!
Then, venom, to thy work.
[Stabs the King.]

entities.title: OSRIC AND LORDS
entities.content:
OSRIC and LORDS.
Treason! treason!

entities.title: KING
entities.content:
KING.
O yet defend me, friends. I am but hurt.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Here, thou incestuous, murderous, damned Dane,
Drink off this potion. Is thy union here?
Follow my mother.
[King dies.]

entities.title: LAERTES
entities.content:
LAERTES.
He is justly serv’d.
It is a poison temper’d by himself.
Exchange forgiveness with me, noble Hamlet.
Mine and my father’s death come not upon thee,
Nor thine on me.
[Dies.]

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
Heaven make thee free of it! I follow thee.
I am dead, Horatio. Wretched Queen, adieu.
You that look pale and tremble at this chance,
That are but mutes or audience to this act,
Had I but time,—as this fell sergeant, death,
Is strict in his arrest,—O, I could tell you,—
But let it be. Horatio, I am dead,
Thou liv’st; report me and my cause aright
To the unsatisfied.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Never believe it.
I am more an antique Roman than a Dane.
Here’s yet some liquor left.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
As th’art a man,
Give me the cup. Let go; by Heaven, I’ll have’t.
O good Horatio, what a wounded name,
Things standing thus unknown, shall live behind me.
If thou didst ever hold me in thy heart,
Absent thee from felicity awhile,
And in this harsh world draw thy breath in pain,
To tell my story.

[March afar off, and shot within.]

What warlike noise is this?

entities.title: OSRIC
entities.content:
OSRIC.
Young Fortinbras, with conquest come from Poland,
To the ambassadors of England gives
This warlike volley.

entities.title: HAMLET
entities.content:
HAMLET.
O, I die, Horatio.
The potent poison quite o’er-crows my spirit:
I cannot live to hear the news from England,
But I do prophesy th’election lights
On Fortinbras. He has my dying voice.
So tell him, with the occurrents more and less,
Which have solicited. The rest is silence.
[Dies.]

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Now cracks a noble heart. Good night, sweet prince,
And flights of angels sing thee to thy rest.
Why does the drum come hither?
[March within.]
Enter Fortinbras, the English Ambassadors and others.

entities.title: FORTINBRAS
entities.content:
FORTINBRAS.
Where is this sight?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
What is it you would see?
If aught of woe or wonder, cease your search.

entities.title: FORTINBRAS
entities.content:
FORTINBRAS.
This quarry cries on havoc. O proud death,
What feast is toward in thine eternal cell,
That thou so many princes at a shot
So bloodily hast struck?

entities.title: FIRST AMBASSADOR
entities.content:
FIRST AMBASSADOR.
The sight is dismal;
And our affairs from England come too late.
The ears are senseless that should give us hearing,
To tell him his commandment is fulfill’d,
That Rosencrantz and Guildenstern are dead.
Where should we have our thanks?

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Not from his mouth,
Had it th’ability of life to thank you.
He never gave commandment for their death.
But since, so jump upon this bloody question,
You from the Polack wars, and you from England
Are here arriv’d, give order that these bodies
High on a stage be placed to the view,
And let me speak to th’ yet unknowing world
How these things came about. So shall you hear
Of carnal, bloody and unnatural acts,
Of accidental judgements, casual slaughters,
Of deaths put on by cunning and forc’d cause,
And, in this upshot, purposes mistook
Fall’n on the inventors’ heads. All this can I
Truly deliver.

entities.title: FORTINBRAS
entities.content:
FORTINBRAS.
Let us haste to hear it,
And call the noblest to the audience.
For me, with sorrow I embrace my fortune.
I have some rights of memory in this kingdom,
Which now to claim my vantage doth invite me.

entities.title: HORATIO
entities.content:
HORATIO.
Of that I shall have also cause to speak,
And from his mouth whose voice will draw on more.
But let this same be presently perform’d,
Even while men’s minds are wild, lest more mischance
On plots and errors happen.

entities.title: FORTINBRAS
entities.content:
FORTINBRAS.
Let four captains
Bear Hamlet like a soldier to the stage,
For he was likely, had he been put on,
To have prov’d most royally; and for his passage,
The soldiers’ music and the rites of war
Speak loudly for him.
Take up the bodies. Such a sight as this
Becomes the field, but here shows much amiss.
Go, bid the soldiers shoot.
[A dead march.]
[Exeunt, bearing off the bodies, after which a peal of ordnance is shot off.]

entities.title: Identity theme
entities.content: The play opens with a question of identity — fitting for a story about uncertainty and deception.

entities.title: Famous soliloquy
entities.content: This is the most famous passage. Note the existential framing — not just suicide but the human condition.

entities.title: Opening tension
entities.content: The play begins with a challenge — 'Who's there?' — establishing uncertainty as the central theme.

entities.title: Supernatural element
entities.content: The ghost appears immediately, grounding the political drama in the supernatural.

entities.title: Montaigne reference
entities.content: Shakespeare's soliloquy echoes Montaigne's Apology for Raymond Sebond, which questions whether man is 'nobler' than beasts.
